name: Two Rivers Bank & Trust
description_short: Two Rivers Bank & Trust is a community-focused FDIC-insured bank offering personal and business banking, mortgages, loans, and wealth management services since 1904.
description_long:
Two Rivers Bank & Trust has operated as a neighborhood bank since 1904, positioning itself as a local financial institution committed to serving both personal and business customers. The bank is FDIC-insured, meaning deposits are backed by the full faith and credit of the U.S. Government up to applicable limits. Currently, the company is undergoing a merger with First Mid Bank & Trust, with different services transitioning at different times according to their website announcement.

The bank offers a comprehensive suite of financial products across personal and business banking. For individuals, Two Rivers provides multiple checking accounts (Essential, Preferred+, Loyalty 60+), savings products (regular savings, money market, CDs, IRAs, HSAs), personal loans, credit cards, and digital banking services including Zelle, mobile wallet, and video tellers. For businesses, they offer various checking and savings accounts, merchant services, ACH, wire transfers, and business loans including SBA loans. Their lending portfolio extends to mortgages (home purchase, construction, home equity), auto loans, student loans, and specialized agriculture financing (real estate, equipment, livestock, FSA-guaranteed loans).

Two Rivers distinguishes itself through specialized agricultural lending products and a stated commitment to community investment and local partnerships. They offer wealth management services through both in-house advisors and Raymond James integration, encompassing investment management, estate planning, retirement planning, and college savings strategies. The bank emphasizes accessibility through multiple digital channels and community-focused messaging ("My Neighborhood. My Bank").

As a traditional bank undergoing merger integration, Two Rivers serves established customers seeking full-service banking with local relationships. However, prospective customers should be aware that the ongoing merger with First Mid Bank & Trust creates transition uncertainty, with services moving to the acquiring institution on different timelines. The bank's emphasis on traditional banking and wealth management services may not appeal to customers seeking fintech-forward solutions or specialized alternative financial products. Many banks offer installment loans with fixed monthly payments, giving borrowers a clear payoff timeline.
services: Business checking and savings accounts with multiple tiers
Certificates of Deposit (CDs) and Money Market Savings
Individual Retirement Accounts (IRAs) and Health Savings Accounts (HSAs)
Personal and business loans, including SBA loans and home equity lines
Specialized agriculture loans (real estate, equipment, livestock, FSA-guaranteed)
Digital banking: Zelle, mobile wallet, video tellers, e-statements
Business services: ACH, wire transfers, remote deposit capture, merchant card services
Wealth management and investment services through in-house advisors and Raymond James
Estate planning and retirement planning consultation
pros: FDIC-insured deposits backed by U.S. Government guarantee
Comprehensive lending options including specialized agriculture loans (livestock, FSA-guaranteed, equipment leasing)
Multiple account tiers with loyalty rewards (Loyalty 60+ Checking)
Integrated wealth management with Raymond James partnership and estate planning services
Digital banking conveniences including Zelle, mobile wallet, video tellers, and remote deposit capture
Business-focused services including Autobooks integration, Two Rivers Treasury Suite, and Positive Pay
Community investment programs and local decision-making since 1904
cons: Ongoing merger with First Mid Bank & Trust creates service transition uncertainty and potential disruption
Limited information about specific interest rates, APRs, or competitive terms on their website
No mention of emergency/payday alternative products or credit-building services for underbanked consumers
Wealth management services likely require minimum account balances not disclosed on website
Geographic availability not clearly specified (appears regional but coverage area undefined)
best_for: Small business owners and farmers seeking local banking relationships with specialized agriculture lending
Individuals with existing customer relationships seeking full-service banking and wealth management
Agricultural enterprises requiring livestock loans, equipment leasing, and FSA-guaranteed financing
High-net-worth individuals interested in estate planning and investment management through Raymond James
diagnosis: Two Rivers Bank & Trust is best for small business owners, farmers, and established consumers seeking traditional full-service banking with local relationships and specialized lending. The primary caveat is the active merger integration with First Mid Bank & Trust, which creates uncertainty about service continuity and timelines—prospective customers should clarify transition schedules before opening new accounts or making major financial commitments.
typical_results_timeline: Account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions vary by product.
meta_description: Two Rivers Bank & Trust is an FDIC-insured bank offering personal and business banking, mortgages, loans, and wealth management services since 1904.
